question.text: Under the SEBI AML Master Circular, which of the following best describes the concept of 'beneficial owner'?

answer.text: The person whose name appears on the account opening form
The broker or intermediary who manages the client's account
The natural person or persons who ultimately own, control or influence a client and/or the person on whose behalf a transaction is being conducted
Any nominee designated by the account holder

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The SEBI Master Circular defines the beneficial owner as the natural person or persons who ultimately own, control or influence a client and/or person on whose behalf a transaction is being conducted. It also incorporates those persons who exercise ultimate effective control over a legal person or arrangement. **The Trap:** Option A confuses the account holder with the beneficial owner — in many cases these may differ, especially with trusts, companies, and nominee arrangements. **Cross-Exam Context:** Beneficial ownership identification is a key topic in NISM Series III-A, III-B, and Series VI. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always look through the corporate structure to identify the natural person(s) at the top — shell companies and complex ownership structures are red flags.
